issues.title: Unclear product vision and business objective
issues.description: The prompt only states a desire to build a 'simple todo list application' without clarifying purpose, success metrics, target platform (web/mobile/desktop), monetization, or business goals. Without this, prioritization and feature trade-offs cannot be made.

issues.title: Target users and roles not specified
issues.description: No information about who will use the app (individuals, teams, enterprises), user roles (admin, user, guest), or expected user workflows. This affects authentication, permissions, UI complexity, and data model.

issues.title: Functional requirements are underspecified
issues.description: Core features are not enumerated. Missing decisions on create/read/update/delete tasks, due dates, reminders, priorities, tags/projects, recurring tasks, search, sorting, collaboration, offline support, import/export, notifications, etc.

issues.title: Technical stack and platform are unspecified
issues.description: No specification of programming language, frameworks (frontend/backend), runtime, or whether this should be a single-page app, native mobile app, or server-rendered site. This directly impacts implementation and developer choices.

issues.title: No data model or persistence choices
issues.description: Entities (task, user, project, tag, comment) and relationships are not defined. Database selection (SQL/NoSQL/local storage) and schema considerations (syncing, offline-first) are missing.

issues.title: Architecture and integrations not defined
issues.description: No description of backend/frontend separation, API contracts, third-party integrations (auth providers, push notifications, calendar sync), or deployment targets (cloud provider, containerization).

issues.title: UI/UX expectations missing
issues.description: No wireframes, required screens, accessibility needs, supported screen sizes, or interaction details (drag-and-drop, keyboard shortcuts). This makes implementing a coherent interface risky.

issues.title: Security, auth, and privacy not addressed
issues.description: No decisions about authentication (email/password, SSO), authorization (role-based access), data encryption, password policies, GDPR or data retention requirements, or handling of sensitive data.

issues.title: Non-functional requirements omitted
issues.description: Performance expectations, availability, scalability, expected number of users, concurrency, and offline behavior are not provided. These influence architecture and operational design.

issues.title: Deliverables, testing, and acceptance criteria absent
issues.description: No definition of deliverables, milestones, QA strategy, unit/integration/end-to-end tests, CI/CD pipeline, documentation, or how success will be accepted and measured.

suggestions: Define the product vision: purpose, target platform (web, iOS, Android, desktop), and success metrics (e.g., MAU, retention, time-to-first-task).
Identify target users and roles: single-user vs. multi-user (teams), admin roles, invited collaborators, and guest access.

Enumerate functional requirements: task CRUD, due dates, reminders/notifications, priorities, tags/projects, recurrence, subtasks, attachments, comments, search, filters, sorting, and sharing/collaboration.
Decide authentication and authorization: anonymous usage, email/password, OAuth (Google/Apple), SSO, and role-based permissions.
Choose a technical stack: frontend framework (React/Vue/Angular/Native), backend language (Node/Python/Go/Ruby), and hosting (serverless, containerized, managed PaaS).
Specify data persistence: entities and basic schema (Task, User, Project, Tag), database type (Postgres, MySQL, Firestore, SQLite), and offline sync requirements.
Design high-level architecture: single-page app with REST/GraphQL API, or server-rendered; decide where business logic lives and any third-party services (Push, Email, Calendar sync).
Provide UI/UX guidelines or wireframes for key screens: task list, task detail/edit, project view, onboarding, settings. List accessibility and responsive requirements.
State non-functional requirements: expected user scale, response-time targets, uptime/SLA, concurrency, and backup/restore needs.
Define security/privacy requirements: encryption at rest/in transit, data retention policy, compliance needs (GDPR/CCPA), and logging/auditing.
Outline testing and QA strategy: unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, code review process, and CI/CD expectations.
Specify deployment and operational requirements: cloud provider, container orchestration (K8s), monitoring, logging, and cost constraints.
List acceptance criteria and milestones: MVP feature set, timeline, and how each feature will be validated.
If collaboration or real-time features are desired, specify real-time expectations (WebSockets, CRDTs, conflict resolution).
Decide on offline support and sync strategy if mobile/offline use is needed (local DB, background sync, conflict handling).
interviewReason: The prompt is too high-level and lacks crucial details that affect architecture and implementation: target users and roles, platform (web/mobile), concrete feature list, technical stack choices, data model and persistence, authentication/authorization, non-functional requirements (scale, performance), and acceptance criteria. These gaps would significantly change design decisions and must be clarified before coding can begin.
